name: Gorilla
tagline: Online experiment builder for behavioral researchers with WebGazer integration
short_impression: Gorilla is a widely-adopted online experiment platform in cognitive neuroscience and psychology research. It offers an intuitive experiment builder with WebGazer eye tracking integration as a control component, allowing researchers to conduct remote studies with sophisticated experimental designs. The platform is known for its timing precision, extensive task templates, and robust participant management system.
pros: Widely used in academic research (high credibility)
Excellent timing precision for cognitive experiments
Large library of validated task templates
Active user community and support forums
cons: Subscription-based pricing (can be expensive for small labs)
Eye tracking is an add-on feature (not included in basic plan)
Primarily designed for cognitive/behavioral research paradigms
Webcam tracking accuracy varies with participant hardware

papers.title: Gorilla in our midst: An online behavioral experiment builder
papers.authors: Anwyl-Irvine, A. L., Massonnié, J., Flitton, A., Kirkham, N., & Evershed, J. K.

claimed_accuracy: Approximately 1-3 degrees (claimed - vendor documentation; varies by setup)
privacy_notes: Data stored on Gorilla cloud infrastructure. GDPR compliant. Institutional data processing agreements available.
